zh.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort是Thomas Fort的女兒，於1364年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾紐波特。她和John William Picton至少有1個女兒。她去世於1427年在她的家鄉享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort是Thomas Fort的女儿，于1364年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾紐波特。她和John William Picton至少有1个女儿。她去世于1427年在她的家乡享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.
German: variant of   Ferch  .

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: Fort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, Walloon, English, and Catalan: nickname from Old French, Middle English, Catalan fort ‘strong, brave’ (from Latin fortis). Compare Lefort .

French: from Fort, a French form of the Latin personal name Fortis (from fortis ‘strong, brave’), chosen by early Christians as a symbol of moral strength and steadfastness, and borne by an obscure saint whose cult was popular during the Middle Ages in southern and southwestern France. Compare St. Fort .
French: habitational name from (Le) Fort, the name of several places in various parts of France, named with Old French fort ‘stronghold’ (from the adjective fort ‘strong’). Compare Lefort .

variants.name: Forte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Carmine, Rocco, Guido, Salvatore, Aldo, Gaetano, Pasquale, Carlo, Matteo, Annamaria.

Italian: from the personal name Forte, from Late Latin fortis ‘strong, brave’ (see Fort 2) or from a short form of a medieval personal name formed with this element, as for example Fortebraccio (‘strong arm’).
Italian: nickname from forte ‘strong’. Compare Lo Forte .

variants.name: Fortin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Lucien, Fernand, Laurent, Pierre, Jacques, Normand, Yvon, Adrien, Alain.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Fort   2, or a diminutive of the nickname   Fort   1 ‘strong, brave’. Compare   Footer  ,   Fortune  , and   Furtaw  .
English: variant of   Fortune  .

variants.name: Dufort
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Cecile, Marcel, Marie Jose.
French:
habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone from Le Fort, the name of several places in various parts of France (see Fort 3).

variants.name: Fuertes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Mario, Angel, Basilio, Carlos, Francisco, Guillermo, Jorge, Raul, Alberto, Amparo.
Spanish:
from an old personal name derived from Latin Fortis, from fortis ‘strong, brave’ (see Fort 2).

variants.name: Lefort
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Julien, Michel, Arsene, Camille, Emile, Etienne, Laurent, Leonce, Ludger, Monique, Normand, Pierre.

French and Walloon: nickname from French le fort ‘the strong’ (see Fort 1). It is also found in Germany, where it is probably of Alsatian origin (compare Lefor ), and in Haiti, where it most likely originates from the nickname Lefort.
French and Walloon: habitational name from Le Fort, the name of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia); see   Fort   3.

variants.name: Fortes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Alfredo, Alberto, Ana, Armando, Arminda, Aurelio, Avelino, Cecelio, Cesar, Diosdado.  Portuguese Joao, Vasco.
Portuguese, Galician, and Spanish:
from an old personal name derived from Latin Fortis, from fortis ‘strong, brave’ (see Fort 2).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort was born in 1364, in Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales as the daughter of Thomas Fort. She had at least 1 daughter with John William Picton. She died in 1427, in her hometown, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort est née en 1364, à Newport, Monmouthshire, Galles, fille de Thomas Fort. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec John William Picton. Elle est décédée en 1427, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort wurde 1364 in Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales, als Tochter von Thomas Fort geboren. Sie hatte mit John William Picton mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1427 im Alter von 63 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort è nata il nel 1364 nel Newport, Monmouthshire, Galles come figlia di Thomas Fort. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con John William Picton. Morì nel 1427, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales: ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアニュープオルト
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fortは1364年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアニュープオルトでThomas Fortの娘として生まれた。彼女はJohn William Pictonとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1427年に故郷で63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort(은)는 1364년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 뉴포트에서 Thomas Fort의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 John William Picton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1427년 그녀의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort nasceu em 1364, em Newport, Monmouthshire, Gales filha de Thomas Fort. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com John William Picton. Ela faleceu em 1427, em sua cidade natal, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort родилась в 1364 г. в местности нюпорт, монмоутшир, Уэльс. Её отца звали Thomas Fort. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от John William Picton. Она умерла в 1427 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Thomas Fort nació en 1364, en Newport, Monmouthshire, Gales como hija de Thomas Fort. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con John William Picton. Murió en 1427, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años.
